Discovering Shelter’s Outdoor Storage Solutions

The page begins with a concise mission: “Upgrade your outdoor storage solutions with Shelter’s innovative products. Our container shelters offer durable protection for your valuable equipment and supplies. Explore Shelter’s range of products today and experience the convenience and durability of our storage solutions.” (tkqlhce)
In other words, Shelter aims to serve the need for robust, flexible outdoor storage — from large equipment to supplies — by offering customizable shelter/product-solutions.

What the Site Offers

From browsing the page, here are some of the main features and offerings:

  • A product catalogue labelled “Shelter” composed of a large number of items (60 products listed) covering two main categories: Container Shelter (17 products) and Storage Shelter (20 products). (tkqlhce)
  • Filtering options stratified by size: width (20 ft, 30 ft, 40 ft, 50 ft, 60 ft), length (20 ft – 100 ft), fabric type (PE, PVC), roof shape (Peak, Arch), door type (Mechanical roll-up, Zipper, Rope pull-up) etc. (tkqlhce)
  • Pricing ranges are visible: e.g., some of the container shelter options list sale prices (e.g., a 20′x20′ “Shipping Container Canopy Shelter” for US$1,699) etc. (tkqlhce)
  • Additional accessories and add-ons: panels, covers, replacements for existing shelters. E.g., “Front Panel with Winch door for W20′ Container Shelter” etc. (tkqlhce)
  • The site has a full e-commerce layout: shopping cart, checkout, shipping policy links, contact information, region/currency settings etc. (tkqlhce)

Why It’s a Good Solution

Here are some of the strengths of what Shelter’s website and product line seem to offer:

  • Wide size range & flexibility: Because the site offers many sizes (20 ft up to 100 ft length, widths up to 60 ft etc), it caters to many use-cases—from modest storage to large-scale equipment.
  • Customisation and accessories: The availability of add-ons (panels, doors, canopy replacements) means one can adapt the basic structure to specific needs (e.g., ventilation, access type, climate protection).
  • Clear product filters: Specifying truss type, roof shape, door type etc helps a buyer sort through options rather than being overwhelmed.
  • Durable outdoor use intent: The fact that the site emphasizes “durable protection for your valuable equipment and supplies” signals that the product is designed for real-world outdoor exposure, not just indoor storage.
  • Transparent pricing and availability: Many items show sale price, stock status (“In stock,” “Pre-order”) which helps set expectations.

Tips for Buyers / Users

If someone is considering using this site or buying a Shelter product, here are some helpful suggestions:

  1. Measure your space carefully – Given the large dimensional range (width, length, height) ensure you have the correct site footprint, overhead clearance and ground conditions for installation.
  2. Select the right roof/door type – For example, if you expect heavy rain or snow, an arch vs peak roof could matter; door type (roll-up vs zipper) affects how you access the shelter.
  3. Consider future expansion/add-ons – Since accessories exist, buy with future adaptation in mind (e.g., installing a winch door panel if you’ll eventually store large vehicles).
  4. Check for local shipping & installation – The site shows free shipping (in some cases) and lists install/FAQ pages — make sure shipping to your region (India/Kerala, in your case) works and what installation support is available. (tkqlhce)
  5. Read warranty & return policies – Large scale outdoor structures are heavy investments; check the “Warranty” and “Returns & Refunds” pages referenced on the site. (tkqlhce)
  6. Verify structural specs if climate demanding – If you’re in a region with heavy winds, hurricanes, cyclones (which in Kerala history we know can happen), check truss gauge, anchoring, fabric strength.

Possible Considerations / Watch-outs

  • Because the site offers many custom options and large sizes, cost can escalate quickly (some items priced over US$10,000+) which may require budgeting accordingly.
  • Shipping to international markets (if you’re in India) might involve duties, long lead-times, local installers — make sure to clarify those.
  • Site lists both “In stock” and “Pre-order” items — lead-times may vary.
  • Site content (based on what we saw) is US-centric (USD pricing, United States region as default) so make sure region/currency matches your market.

The Overall Vibe & Customer Experience

The website gives a professional, industrial-grade vibe—targeting serious outdoor storage needs (not just home-garden sheds). The product imagery, large size options, and detailed filters convey that this is for warehouses, equipment yards, commercial use, or large residential estates.
User-experience wise, it seems well structured with clear navigation (Shop, New Products, Sale, filters) and contact details. The offer of free shipping (noted near top: “Free shipping (Excluding AS-IS and CLEARANCE)”) helps reduce friction. (tkqlhce)

Final Thoughts

If you’re looking for an outdoor storage solution that goes beyond a simple shed — something that offers significant size, durability, and flexibility — the “Shelter” line on Chery Industrial’s site is worth exploring. Because you’re based in Kannur, Kerala, I’d suggest paying extra attention to: shipping logistics, installation support, anchor/foundation requirements (especially for monsoon/hurricane-type weather), and how well the local climate suits the fabric/roof choices.
If you like, I can check for Indian distributors or local availability of these models (or similar) to avoid heavy overseas shipping; would that be helpful?
